Enzyme_Name.reason: Title/keywords and text identify KCS1 as 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase 1.

Source_Species.reason: KCS1 coding sequence was amplified from an Arabidopsis cDNA library.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana is the species used for KCS1 constructs and mutants.

Applied_Species.reason: KCS1 was experimentally studied in Arabidopsis, and KCS-family expression/pathway relevance was examined in cotton fibres.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana and Gossypium hirsutum both appear in the paper as experimental systems related to KCS1/KCS-family analyses.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Cotton variety Coker 312 is explicitly given; no Arabidopsis variety is specified.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Paper places KCS1 in the KCS family and states it has a transmembrane domain; FAE1/KCS terminology is consistent with the KCS family context.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: KCS1 is discussed as a key enzyme in VLCFA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: KCS1 is described as part of fatty acid elongation/VLCFA biosynthesis, i.e. the elongase module.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: KCS1 is presented as key/critical, but the paper does not explicitly call it the committed step.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper identifies KCS1 as a KCS enzyme in VLCFA biosynthesis, but does not explicitly describe the condensation of malonyl-CoA with long-chain acyl-CoA in this article.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Specific substrates long-chain acyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA are not stated in the paper text.

Primary_Product.reason: Elongated VLCFA intermediates are inferable from KCS function, but 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates are not explicitly named in the paper.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results directly connect KCS1 overexpression/co-overexpression with increased VLCFA content and ratio to short-chain fatty acids.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: VLCFAs are directly measured; C24:0 is specifically highlighted as increased in cotton fibres.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper states VLCFAs are required for fibre elongation/development and discusses membrane expansion and signalling roles.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: VLCFAs were measured in cotton fibres and Arabidopsis seeds.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Arabidopsis KCS1 overexpression and AKR2A/KCS1 co-overexpression increased oil, VLCFA content, and VLCFA/short-chain FA ratio; co-overexpression gave the highest increase.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are stated in Results: AKR2A-KCS1 interaction, KCS1 overexpression lines with increased oil/VLCFA, and highest increase in co-overexpressors.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper calls KCS1 a critical/key enzyme, but does not directly demonstrate or state rate-limiting control.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Membrane association/transmembrane domain is supported, but specific localization to the endomembrane system is not explicitly stated here.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: KCS1 transcript and protein were much higher in KCS1-overexpressing and AKR2A/KCS1 co-overexpressing Arabidopsis plants.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Cotton KCS genes were highly upregulated during fibre development, with highest fold increases around 15 DPA in AKR2A-overexpressing lines.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: KCS-related changes were assessed across fibre developmental stages, especially 15 DPA, and under transgenic overexpression.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all reported: overexpression/co-overexpression, RNA blot/qRT-PCR, Western blot, GC-MS, yeast two-hybrid, and co-IP.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: AKR2A-KCS1 interaction was shown in vitro and in vivo.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Constructs used 35S-driven expression from pBI121-based vectors; cotton AKR2A construct explicitly used CaMV 35S and Arabidopsis vectors are 35S-based.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering potential is discussed more for AKR2A than specifically for KCS1; KCS1 as a target is plausible but not explicitly emphasized as an application target.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq of cotton fibre was performed in this study; no accession is reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects that direct KCS1 evidence is strongest in Arabidopsis, whereas cotton evidence is mainly KCS-family transcript/pathway level.

corrections.reason: Specific substrates long-chain acyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA are not stated in the paper text.

Source_Species.reason: AKR2A cDNA was amplified from an Arabidopsis cDNA library.

Applied_Species.reason: AKR2A was overexpressed in cotton and also studied in Arabidopsis overexpression and mutant lines.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Gossypium hirsutum and Arabidopsis thaliana are both explicit in the experiments.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Cotton variety Coker 312 is explicitly stated; Arabidopsis variety is not specified.

Regulator_Type.reason: AKR2A is referred to as a molecular chaperone, and Discussion proposes it may act as a molecular scaffold.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AKR2A is an ankyrin repeat-containing protein, and the PEST motif is specifically mentioned in the interaction analysis.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results and summary directly state AKR2A regulates VLCFA biosynthesis and downstream ethylene/auxin/H2O2-related processes.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Paper reports positive regulation of VLCFAs, AKR2A interaction with KCS1, and upregulation of VLCFA-, ethylene-, auxin-, H2O2-, cell wall-, and cytoskeleton-related genes.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: Many listed genes/groups are supported (KCS1/2/6/12/13, KCRs, ECRs, ACOs, GhPIN3a/b, APX1, POD, EXP1, TUB1, ACT1, CER6, LCB1), but the exact complete list including ACS as a discrete gene target is not fully enumerated in the paper text.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: RNA-seq/qRT-PCR support upregulation of VLCFA biosynthetic and downstream ethylene, auxin transport, ROS homeostasis, and fibre elongation genes.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Interaction with KCS1 is supported, but involvement in a fatty acid elongase complex is proposed as a model rather than directly demonstrated.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: VLCFAs, especially C24:0, were directly measured and increased in AKR2A-overexpressing lines.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Paper links VLCFAs to cotton fibre elongation/development and discusses roles in membrane expansion and signalling.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Measured in cotton fibres and Arabidopsis seeds.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: AKR2A overexpression increased VLCFA content and ratio and promoted fibre elongation/fibre quality traits; mutants showed opposite trends in Arabidopsis seeds.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Overexpression increased fibre initiation, elongation, fibre length, uniformity and strength, and increased VLCFA/oil; mutants had reduced oil/VLCFAs.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary statements are directly supported by Results and Summary.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are explicitly described in the study.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Data were collected across fibre stages 0-25 DPA, especially 10-15 DPA, in field and in vitro ovule culture conditions.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: qRT-PCR showed AKR2A transcript was >50-fold higher than WT between 10 and 15 DPA in transgenic cotton.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Cotton construct explicitly used CaMV 35S; Arabidopsis constructs were 35S-based pBI121 vectors.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: KCS1 interaction was demonstrated by yeast two-hybrid, pull-down/co-IP.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Summary and Discussion explicitly present AKR2A as a potential candidate gene for improving fibre yield and quality through genetic engineering.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Paper states no obvious phenotype in transgenic cotton and chlorotic seedling leaves in Arabidopsis akr2a mutants.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq of 15 DPA cotton fibres was performed; no accession is reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Accurately summarizes that the source gene is Arabidopsis, applied phenotype is mainly in cotton, and authors propose AKR2A acts upstream of ethylene/auxin/H2O2 via VLCFAs.
